headline: AI reshaping job market: Entry-level dev hiring down 20%, but South Florida tech scene heats up with new investment waves

stories.title: Stanford Data: Entry-Level Dev Jobs Down 20% Due to AI
stories.summary: Stanford economists revealed entry-level software developer hiring for workers aged 22-26 is down 20%, and call center roles dropped 15%—these are current numbers, not projections. Meanwhile, new AI-adjacent roles are actually growing, so adaptation is key.
stories.takeaway: AI isn't killing all jobs—it's reshaping them. Workers who use AI to level up their skills are seeing growth, fr.

stories.title: Tech Giants Spending $650B on AI, South Florida Poised to Benefit Indirectly
stories.summary: Amazon, Google, and Meta are collectively dropping hundreds of billions on AI infrastructure. While most spending concentrates in existing tech hubs, South Florida could capture talent demand, construction gigs, and engineering services fallout.
stories.takeaway: South Florida's emerging tech corridor could lowkey ride the coattails of Big Tech's massive AI spending wave.

stories.title: South Florida Tech Hub Pulse: Region Eyes 3-5 IPOs in Next 60 Months
stories.summary: At Tech Hub Pulse, leaders predicted a surge in space and defense tech investment. With Brickell becoming a financial hub and venture capital flooding in, South Florida could see 3-5 major IPOs over the next five years if infrastructure and talent recruitment keep pace.
stories.takeaway: South Florida is literally positioning itself to be the next big thing in AI and emerging tech—tbh, the ecosystem is ready.

stories.title: AI Transitioning From Experimental to Mainstream in Florida Industries
stories.summary: Healthcare, tourism, and manufacturing leaders at Florida's Economic Outlook summit agree AI is shifting from experimental pet projects to core business strategy in 2026. Workforce adaptation and regulatory challenges are the biggest concerns.
stories.takeaway: AI adoption is getting real across Florida industries—companies gotta figure out workforce training ASAP.

watchNext.title: BLS Employment Projections: Software Dev Jobs Expected to Grow 17.9% Through 2033
watchNext.why: Despite entry-level hiring slowdowns, long-term projections show software developers are still in demand. Good news for career planners—just gotta survive the next few years.

watchNext.title: Harvard Research: ChatGPT Killed 13% of Routine Jobs, Boosted Creative Roles 20%
watchNext.why: New study shows routine automation-prone roles collapsed 13%, but analytical and creative job openings jumped 20%—generative AI is literally reshaping what skills matter.
